summary: c=2 native batch full-attention score-scaling cleanup: the value-parallel path now applies inv_denom while reading scores instead of rewriting normalized scores first. Correctness remains green and profiler context-kernel time improves slightly, but end-to-end projection-check throughput is neutral/noisy and c2 remains below c1; no retained c2 claim.

software.hipcc_version:
HIP version: 7.2.53211-364a905
AMD clang version 22.0.0git (/srcdest/rocm-llvm f58b06dce1f9c15707c5f808fd002e18c2accf7e)
Target: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu
Thread model: posix
InstalledDir: /opt/rocm/lib/llvm/bin

code_change.description: In the long-context value-parallel branch, skip the separate scores[token] *= inv_denom shared-memory writeback and apply inv_denom in the value accumulation read. The value_groups==1 primitive/c1-style branch is unchanged.

rejected_alternatives.name: parallel_softmax_writeback
rejected_alternatives.evidence: Dirty c2 primary run stayed generated-token green but slowed to 110.080 tok/s aggregate, below the kept value-scan baseline; not retained.

rejected_alternatives.name: 512_thread_value_groups
rejected_alternatives.evidence: Dirty c2 primary run stayed generated-token green but slowed to 107.328 tok/s aggregate; 1024-thread c2 launch retained.

conclusion.reason: The score-scaling cleanup preserves c2 generated-token equality and slightly reduces the traced native batch context-kernel duration to 0.994x of the prior value-scan artifact. Primary default verify improved to 1.016x prior, but projection-check throughput was neutral/noisy (best 0.999x, mean 0.989x prior) and remains below c1; no retained c2 performance claim.
